sections.label: Section 380
sections.original: οὕτω γὰρ ἤρξατʼ , ἄνομα μὲν τεκεῖν ἐμέ ,
sections.translation: So it began , my childbearing was unholy , and in an evil hour I married your father and you were born . But why repeat these horrors ? What the gods send we have to bear . I am afraid to ask you what I would , for fear of stinging your heart ; yet I long to .

sections.label: Section 385
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐξερώτα , μηδὲν ἐνδεὲς λίπῃς ·
sections.translation: No , question me , leave out nothing ; for your will , mother , is my pleasure too .

sections.label: Section 387
sections.original: καὶ δή σʼ ἐρωτῶ πρῶτον ὧν χρῄζω τυχεῖν ,
sections.translation: Well then , first I ask you what I long to have answered . What is it , to be deprived of one ’ s country ? Is it a great evil ?

sections.label: Section 389
sections.original: μέγιστον · ἔργῳ δʼ ἐστὶ μεῖζον ἢ λόγῳ .
sections.translation: The greatest ; harder to bear than tell .

sections.label: Section 390
sections.original: τίς ὁ τρόπος αὐτοῦ ; τί φυγάσιν τὸ δυσχερές ;
sections.translation: What is it like ? What annoys the exile ?

sections.label: Section 391
sections.original: ἓν μὲν μέγιστον , οὐκ ἔχει παρρησίαν .
sections.translation: One thing most of all ; he cannot speak his mind .

sections.label: Section 392
sections.original: δούλου τόδʼ εἶπας , μὴ λέγειν ἅ τις φρονεῖ .
sections.translation: This is a slave ’ s lot you speak of , not to say what one thinks .

sections.label: Section 393
sections.original: τὰς τῶν κρατούντων ἀμαθίας φέρειν χρεών .
sections.translation: The follies of the rulers must be borne .

sections.label: Section 394
sections.original: καὶ τοῦτο λυπρόν , συνασοφεῖν τοῖς μὴ σοφοῖς .
sections.translation: That too is painful , to join in the folly of fools .

sections.label: Section 395
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐς τὸ κέρδος παρὰ φύσιν δουλευτέον .
sections.translation: Yet to gain our ends we must serve against our nature .

sections.label: Section 396
sections.original: αἱ δʼ ἐλπίδες βόσκουσι φυγάδας , ὡς λόγος .
sections.translation: Hope , they say , is the exile ’ s food .

sections.label: Section 397
sections.original: καλοῖς βλέπουσαί γʼ ὄμμασιν , μέλλουσι δέ .
sections.translation: Yes , hope that looks so fair ; but always in the future .

sections.label: Section 398
sections.original: οὐδʼ ὁ χρόνος αὐτὰς διεσάφησʼ οὔσας κενάς ;
sections.translation: But doesn ’ t time expose its emptiness ?

sections.label: Section 399
sections.original: ἔχουσιν Ἀφροδίτην τινʼ ἡδεῖαν κακῶν .
sections.translation: It has a certain winsome charm in misfortune .

sections.label: Section 400
sections.original: πόθεν δʼ ἐβόσκου , πρὶν γάμοις εὑρεῖν βίον ;
sections.translation: Where did you get your living , before your marriage found it for you ?

sections.label: Section 401
sections.original: ποτὲ μὲν ἐπʼ ἦμαρ εἶχον , εἶτʼ οὐκ εἶχον ἄν .
sections.translation: Sometimes I would have enough for the day , and sometimes not .

sections.label: Section 402
sections.original: φίλοι δὲ πατρὸς καὶ ξένοι σʼ οὐκ ὠφέλουν ;
sections.translation: Didn ’ t your father ’ s friends and guests assist you ?

sections.label: Section 403
sections.original: εὖ πρᾶσσε · τὰ φίλων δʼ οὐδέν , ἤν τι δυστυχῇς .
sections.translation: Seek to be prosperous ; friends are nothing in misfortune .

sections.label: Section 404
sections.original: οὐδʼ ηὑγένειά σʼ ἦρεν εἰς ὕψος μέγαν ;
sections.translation: Didn ’ t your noble breeding lead you to the heights ?

sections.label: Section 405
sections.original: κακὸν τὸ μὴ ἔχειν · τὸ γένος οὐκ ἔβοσκέ με .
sections.translation: Poverty is a curse ; breeding did not find me food .

sections.label: Section 406
sections.original: ἡ πατρίς , ὡς ἔοικε , φίλτατον βροτοῖς .
sections.translation: Man ’ s dearest treasure , it seems , is his country .

sections.label: Section 407
sections.original: οὐδʼ ὀνομάσαι δύναιʼ ἂν ὡς ἐστὶν φίλον .
sections.translation: You could not name how dear it is !

sections.label: Section 408
sections.original: πῶς δʼ ἦλθες Ἄργος ; τίνʼ ἐπίνοιαν ἔσχεθες ;
sections.translation: How did you come to Argos ? What was your scheme ?

sections.label: Section 409
sections.original: ἔχρησʼ Ἀδράστῳ Λοξίας χρησμόν τινα .
sections.translation: Loxias gave Adrastus an oracle .

sections.label: Section 410
sections.original: ποῖον ; τί τοῦτʼ ἔλεξας ; οὐκ ἔχω μαθεῖν .
sections.translation: What was it ? What are you saying ? I cannot guess .

sections.label: Section 411
sections.original: κάπρῳ λέοντί θʼ ἁρμόσαι παίδων γάμους .
sections.translation: That he should marry his daughters to a boar and a lion .

sections.label: Section 412
sections.original: καὶ σοὶ τί θηρῶν ὀνόματος μετῆν , τέκνον ;
sections.translation: What did you , my son , have to do with the name of beasts ?

sections.label: Section 413
sections.original: οὐκ οἶδʼ · ὁ δαίμων μʼ ἐκάλεσεν πρὸς τὴν τύχην .
sections.translation: I don ’ t know ; the deity summoned me there to my destiny .

sections.label: Section 414
sections.original: σοφὸς γὰρ ὁ θεός · τίνι τρόπῳ δʼ ἔσχες λέχος ;
sections.translation: Yes , for the god is wise ; but how did you win your wife ?

sections.label: Section 415
sections.original: νὺξ ἦν , Ἀδράστου δʼ ἦλθον ἐς παραστάδας .
sections.translation: It was night when I reached the porch of Adrastus .

sections.label: Section 416
sections.original: κοίτας ματεύων , ᾗ φυγὰς πλανώμενος ;
sections.translation: In search of a resting-place , because you were in exile ?

sections.label: Section 417
sections.original: ἦν ταῦτα · κᾆτά γʼ ἦλθεν ἄλλος αὖ φυγάς .
sections.translation: Yes ; and then another exile came there .

sections.label: Section 418
sections.original: τίς οὗτος ; ὡς ἄρʼ ἄθλιος κἀκεῖνος ἦν .
sections.translation: Who was he ? He too was in trouble , surely .

sections.label: Section 419
sections.original: Τυδεύς , ὃν Οἰνέως φασὶν ἐκφῦναι πατρός .
sections.translation: Tydeus ; they say that Oineus is his father .

sections.label: Section 420
sections.original: τί θηρσὶν ὑμᾶς δῆτʼ Ἄδραστος ᾔκασεν ;
sections.translation: But why did Adrastus compare you to wild beasts ?

sections.label: Section 421
sections.original: στρωμνῆς ἐς ἀλκὴν οὕνεκʼ ἤλθομεν πέρι .
sections.translation: Because we came to blows about our bed .

sections.label: Section 422
sections.original: ἐνταῦθα Ταλαοῦ παῖς συνῆκε θέσφατα ;
sections.translation: Was it then that the son of Talaus understood the oracle ?

sections.label: Section 423
sections.original: κἄδωκέ γʼ ἡμῖν δύο δυοῖν νεάνιδας .
sections.translation: Yes , and he gave the two of us his two daughters .

sections.label: Section 424
sections.original: ἆρʼ εὐτυχεῖς οὖν τοῖς γάμοις ἢ δυστυχεῖς ;
sections.translation: Are you blessed or cursed in your marriage ?

sections.label: Section 425
sections.original: οὐ μεμπτὸς ἡμῖν ὁ γάμος ἐς τόδʼ ἡμέρας .
sections.translation: As yet I have no fault to find with it .

sections.label: Section 426
sections.original: πῶς δʼ ἐξέπεισας δεῦρό σοι σπέσθαι στρατόν ;
sections.translation: How did you persuade an army to follow you here ?

sections.label: Section 427
sections.original: δισσοῖς Ἄδραστος ὤμοσεν γαμβροῖς τόδε ,
sections.translation: Adrastus swore an oath to his two sons-in-law , Tydeus and myself ; for he is my relative by marriage that he would restore us both to our country , me first .

sections.label: Section 430
sections.original: πολλοὶ δὲ Δαναῶν καὶ Μυκηναίων ἄκροι
sections.translation: So many Danaan and Mycenaean chiefs have joined me , doing me a bitter though needful service , for it is against my own city I am marching . Now I call the gods to witness , that it is not willingly I have raised the spear against my willing friends .

sections.label: Section 435
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἐς σὲ τείνει τῶνδε διάλυσις κακῶν ,
sections.translation: But it belongs to you , mother , to dissolve this unhappy feud , and , by reconciling loving brothers , to end the trouble for me and you and the whole city . It has been said for a long time , but I will say it anyway : wealth is most valued by men ,

sections.label: Section 440
sections.original: δύναμίν τε πλείστην τῶν ἐν ἀνθρώποις ἔχει .
sections.translation: and of all things in the world it has the greatest power . This I have come to secure at the head of my great army ; for a man well-born but poor is worth nothing .

sections.label: Section 443
sections.original: καὶ μὴν Ἐτεοκλῆς ἐς διαλλαγὰς ὅδε
sections.translation: And see , Eteocles comes here to discuss the truce . It is your task , mother Jocasta , to speak

sections.label: Section 445
sections.original: τοιούσδε μύθους οἷς διαλλάξεις τέκνα .
sections.translation: such words as may reconcile your sons .

sections.label: Section 446
sections.original: μῆτερ , πάρειμι · τὴν χάριν δὲ σοὶ διδοὺς
sections.translation: Mother , I am here ; I have come to do you a favor . What am I to do ? Let some one begin the conference ; for I stopped marshalling the citizens in pairs of companies around the walls , so that I might hear your

sections.label: Section 450
sections.original: κοινὰς βραβείας , αἷς ὑπόσπονδον μολεῖν
sections.translation: arbitration between us , by which you persuaded me to admit this man under truce within the walls .

sections.label: Section 452
sections.original: ἐπίσχες · οὔτοι τὸ ταχὺ τὴν δίκην ἔχει ,
sections.translation: Wait ; haste does not carry justice with it ; but slow deliberation often attains a wise result . Restrain the fierceness of your look and panting rage ;

sections.label: Section 455
sections.original: οὐ γὰρ τὸ λαιμότμητον εἰσορᾷς κάρα
sections.translation: for this is not the Gorgon ’ s severed head but your own brother whom you see has come . You too , Polyneices , turn and face your brother ; for if you look at him , you will speak and listen to him the better .

sections.label: Section 460
sections.original: παραινέσαι δὲ σφῷν τι βούλομαι σοφόν ·
sections.translation: I want to give you both one piece of good counsel ; when a man that is angry with his friend confronts him face to face , he ought only to keep in view the object of his coming , forgetting all previous quarrels .

sections.label: Section 465
sections.original: λόγος μὲν οὖν σὸς πρόσθε , Πολύνεικες τέκνον ·
sections.translation: My son Polyneices , speak first , for you have come at the head of a Danaid army , alleging wrongful treatment ; may some god be the judge and reconciler of the troubles .

sections.label: Section 469
sections.original: ἁπλοῦς ὁ μῦθος τῆς ἀληθείας ἔφυ ,
sections.translation: The words of truth are naturally simple ,

sections.label: Section 470
sections.original: κοὐ ποικίλων δεῖ τἄνδιχʼ ἑρμηνευμάτων ·
sections.translation: and justice needs no subtle interpretations , for it has a fitness in itself ; but the words of injustice , being sick in themselves , require clever treatment . I provided for his interests and mine in our father ’ s house , being anxious to escape the curse

sections.label: Section 475
sections.original: ἃς Οἰδίπους ἐφθέγξατʼ εἰς ἡμᾶς ποτε ,
sections.translation: which Oedipus once uttered against us ; of my own free-will I left this land , allowing him to rule the country for one full year , on condition that I should then take up the rule in turn , instead of plunging into deadly enmity with this man ,

sections.label: Section 480
sections.original: κακόν τι δρᾶσαι καὶ παθεῖν , ἃ γίγνεται .
sections.translation: doing others harm and suffering it myself , as is now the case . But he , after consenting to this and calling the gods to witness his oath , has performed none of his promises , but is still keeping the sovereignty in his own hands together with my share of our heritage .

sections.label: Section 484
sections.original: καὶ νῦν ἕτοιμός εἰμι τἀμαυτοῦ λαβὼν
sections.translation: And now I am ready to take my own

sections.label: Section 485
sections.original: στρατὸν μὲν ἔξω τῆσδʼ ἀποστεῖλαι χθονός ,
sections.translation: and dismiss the army from this land , receiving my house in turn to dwell in , and once more restore it to him for an equal period , instead of ravaging our country and bringing scaling-ladders against the towers ,

sections.label: Section 490
sections.original: ἃ μὴ κυρήσας τῆς δίκης πειράσομαι
sections.translation: as I shall attempt to do if I do not get my rights . I call the gods to witness that spite of my just dealing in everything I am being unjustly robbed of my country , a most unholy act . I have made my points , mother , without stringing together
